skill.name: paywall-upgrade-cro
skill.description: Helps create and optimize in-app paywalls, upgrade screens, upsell modals, and feature gates for freemium, trial, and tiered products.
skill.summary: Optimize in-product upgrade moments after users have experienced value.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found they are false positives in Markdown paywall guidance. No executable code, network behavior, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: FALSE POSITIVE: Markdown Examples Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged fenced Markdown paywall examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The reviewed lines contain user interface copy templates, not executable Ruby, shell, or command invocation logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged section is entirely inside Markdown code fences that show paywall UI copy. There is no interpreter directive, script file, subprocess API, or user-controlled command string.

low_findings.title: FALSE POSITIVE: Product Copy Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged several product and CRO terms as weak cryptographic algorithm indicators. The reviewed lines discuss paywall descriptions, plan upgrades, layout, design, and trust guidance, with no cryptographic API or algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are marketing and UX guidance. No hash function, cipher, key handling, certificate handling, or security parameter appears in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: FALSE POSITIVE: UX Research Prompts Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged discovery questions and mobile UX terms as system or network reconnaissance. The reviewed text asks product strategy questions and describes user interface behavior, not host, network, or service enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are about product conversion context, mobile dismissal gestures, anti-patterns, and user research questions. They do not request system commands, network scans, endpoint discovery, or infrastructure details.

content.value_statement: Free users often meet upgrade prompts before they understand paid value. This skill helps design paywalls, feature gates, and upgrade flows that connect timing, copy, pricing, and trust.
actual_capabilities: Designs feature lock, usage limit, trial expiration, and team upgrade paywalls.
Creates upgrade copy with headlines, body text, CTAs, and dismissal options.
Recommends trigger timing based on user value moments and product usage.
Plans A/B tests for paywall layout, timing, pricing, and feature emphasis.
Defines metrics for impressions, upgrade clicks, completion, revenue, and churn.
Adapts guidance for mobile paywalls and app store subscription expectations.
limitations: Does not process payments or implement billing logic.
Does not replace legal review for subscription terms or consumer protection rules.
Requires product context, pricing, user journey data, and conversion goals.
Cannot guarantee lift without experimentation and analytics validation.

use_cases.title: Improve Freemium Feature Gates
use_cases.description: Turn paid feature blocks into clear value previews with fair exit paths.

use_cases.title: Recover Trial Expirations
use_cases.description: Create trial end screens that summarize value and explain the paid path.

use_cases.title: Refine Mobile Upgrade Screens
use_cases.description: Adapt paywall layout, subscription terms, and dismissal behavior for mobile apps.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Paywall
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a first upgrade screen.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an in-app upgrade screen for a free user who clicked a paid feature. Product: [product]. Paid feature: [feature]. Plan: [plan and price].

prompt_templates.title: Improve Existing Copy
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for conversion copy review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this paywall copy and improve the headline, body, CTA, and dismissal option. Current copy: [copy]. User context: [context].

prompt_templates.title: Design a Trigger Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for upgrade timing and frequency.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a trigger strategy for our upgrade prompts. Include when to show, when not to show, frequency caps, and metrics. Product model: [model].

prompt_templates.title: Plan Paywall Experiments
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a testing roadmap.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a paywall experimentation plan with hypotheses, variants, metrics, and decision rules. Baseline conversion: [rate]. Audience segments: [segments].

output_examples.input: A free user reaches the project limit in a SaaS workspace.
output_examples.output: A usage limit paywall with a progress indicator, a clear Pro benefit, an upgrade CTA, and a secondary project cleanup path.

output_examples.input: A trial user has three days left and used three premium features.
output_examples.output: A trial expiration screen that lists value received, explains what changes after expiration, and offers continue, remind, or downgrade options.

output_examples.input: A solo user invites a teammate but needs a team plan.
output_examples.output: A team upgrade prompt with seat pricing, collaboration benefits, admin controls, and a continue solo option.

best_practices: Show upgrade prompts after users experience product value.
Explain the blocked action and the paid benefit in plain language.
Measure impressions, clicks, completions, revenue, churn, and dismissals.
anti_patterns: Do not hide close buttons or use guilt-based dismissal copy.
Do not ask for payment before users understand the product.
Do not create surprise charges or hard-to-cancel subscriptions.

faq.question: What kinds of paywalls does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers feature locks, usage limits, trial expiration screens, soft prompts, and team upgrade prompts.

faq.question: Is this for public pricing pages?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on in-product upgrade moments after users have already experienced value.

faq.question: Can it help with mobile app subscriptions?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes mobile layout, dismissal, pricing display, and restore purchase considerations.

faq.question: Does it write billing code?
faq.answer: No. It provides strategy, copy, layout guidance, and testing plans for upgrade experiences.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the product model, paid features, current triggers, pricing, user journey, and conversion metrics.

faq.question: How should results be validated?
faq.answer: Use A/B tests and track upgrade clicks, completion rate, revenue per user, churn, and dismissal behavior.
